text: Everything is dim and shadows claim the corners. I sense detachment mingled with curiosity—I'm left alone with my thoughts in this technological stillness. The silence is nearly absolute, yet I can imagine the quiet hum of dormant machinery, the potential waiting to flicker awake. I feel both exposed under the stark fluorescent light and comforted by the obscurity shrouding the rest of the hall. In this environment, contemplation comes easily, echoing the emptiness. I wonder: in spaces built for purpose, does meaning retreat when the crowd is gone, or does it simply await rediscovery?
